description: The format of bytes returned by SigningBackend::sign() (fixed-size vs DER vs other) is a per-algorithm contract that is nowhere stated in the trait docs or in SigningAlgorithm. Each adapter (Ed25519Signer, EcdsaP256Signer, RsaPssSigner) silently assumes fixed-size encoding and calls from_slice(). Backend implementors have no contract to follow. A future backend that returns DER (AWS KMS, Azure KV both return DER for ECDSA) will compile and pass unit tests against a mock, then fail in integration with a cryptic signature::Error at runtime. The SigningBackend trait docs must specify the expected byte format per algorithm, or the adapters must document their assumption and reject DER explicitly.
title: sign() byte-format contract not part of SigningBackend trait
